task.name: Stroop (RDoC)
reference_literature: Hedge et al. (2018) - online Stroop RT distributions
Pratte et al. (2010) - ex-Gaussian Stroop parameters

stimuli.description: Color word whose meaning matches ink color

detection.selector: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('.stroop-stim'); if (!el) return false; const word = (el.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase(); const c = (el.style.color || '').replace(/\s/g,''); const map = {'rgb(255,112,112)':'red','rgb(112,112,255)':'blue','rgb(112,255,112)':'green'}; return map[c] && map[c] === word; })()

response.response_key_js: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('.stroop-stim'); if (!el) return null; const c = (el.style.color || '').replace(/\s/g,''); const colorMap = {'rgb(255,112,112)':'red','rgb(112,112,255)':'blue','rgb(112,255,112)':'green'}; const colorName = colorMap[c]; if (!colorName) return null; try { const pr = window.possibleResponses; if (pr && pr.length >= 3) { const keyToColor = {}; keyToColor[pr[0][1]] = 'red'; keyToColor[pr[1][1]] = 'blue'; keyToColor[pr[2][1]] = 'green'; for (const k of Object.keys(keyToColor)) { if (keyToColor[k] === colorName) return k; } } } catch(e){} const hexMap = {'red':'#ff7070','blue':'#7070ff','green':'#70ff70'}; const targetHex = hexMap[colorName]; const spans = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.prompt_box span, .list-text span, span.large')); for (const sp of spans) { const sc = (sp.style.color || '').toLowerCase().replace(/\s/g,''); if (sc === targetHex || sc === 'rgb(255,112,112)' && colorName==='red' || sc === 'rgb(112,112,255)' && colorName==='blue' || sc === 'rgb(112,255,112)' && colorName==='green') { const parent = sp.closest('p, li'); if (!parent) continue; const txt = parent.textContent; const m = txt.match(/([,./])/); if (m) return m[1]; } } return null; })()

stimuli.description: Color word whose meaning differs from ink color

detection.selector: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('.stroop-stim'); if (!el) return false; const word = (el.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase(); const c = (el.style.color || '').replace(/\s/g,''); const map = {'rgb(255,112,112)':'red','rgb(112,112,255)':'blue','rgb(112,255,112)':'green'}; return map[c] && map[c] !== word; })()

response.response_key_js: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('.stroop-stim'); if (!el) return null; const c = (el.style.color || '').replace(/\s/g,''); const colorMap = {'rgb(255,112,112)':'red','rgb(112,112,255)':'blue','rgb(112,255,112)':'green'}; const colorName = colorMap[c]; if (!colorName) return null; try { const pr = window.possibleResponses; if (pr && pr.length >= 3) { const keyToColor = {}; keyToColor[pr[0][1]] = 'red'; keyToColor[pr[1][1]] = 'blue'; keyToColor[pr[2][1]] = 'green'; for (const k of Object.keys(keyToColor)) { if (keyToColor[k] === colorName) return k; } } } catch(e){} const hexMap = {'red':'#ff7070','blue':'#7070ff','green':'#70ff70'}; const targetHex = hexMap[colorName]; const spans = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.prompt_box span, .list-text span, span.large')); for (const sp of spans) { const sc = (sp.style.color || '').toLowerCase().replace(/\s/g,''); if (sc === targetHex || sc === 'rgb(255,112,112)' && colorName==='red' || sc === 'rgb(112,112,255)' && colorName==='blue' || sc === 'rgb(112,255,112)' && colorName==='green') { const parent = sp.closest('p, li'); if (!parent) continue; const txt = parent.textContent; const m = txt.match(/([,./])/); if (m) return m[1]; } } return null; })()

task_specific.notes: Key-color mapping is counterbalanced via group_index. The mapping is resolved at runtime via window.possibleResponses (pr[0]=red, pr[1]=blue, pr[2]=green; each entry is [finger, key, label]). Keys are always ',' '.' '/' but mapped to different colors across participants.

autocorrelation.rationale: Low-to-moderate lag-1 RT autocorrelation typical of choice RT tasks (Laming, 1968; Gilden, 2001).

fatigue_drift.rationale: Short task (~120 test trials + practice); mild drift expected over session.

post_error_slowing.rationale: Rabbitt (1966); Danielmeier & Ullsperger (2011) - ~30-80 ms post-error slowing in Stroop-like tasks.

condition_repetition.rationale: Gratton effect / conflict adaptation: faster RT when previous trial shared congruency (Gratton et al., 1992).

pink_noise.rationale: 1/f structure in RT series (Gilden, 2001; Wagenmakers et al., 2004).

post_interrupt_slowing.rationale: No interrupt/stop signal in this task.

between_subject_jitter.rationale: Between-subject RT variability in Stroop is substantial (Hedge et al., 2018); online samples show slightly wider spread.

pilot.rationale: Practice block has 4 trials (2 congruent, 2 incongruent). The pilot stalled on an instructions page that jsPsych renders with only clickable nav (allow_keys is false for individual instruction pages). The advance_behavior must click #jspsych-instructions-next to progress past each instruction page; Enter alone will not advance those pages. The initial feedback_instruct page DOES accept Enter.

phase_detection.feedback: (() => { if (document.querySelector('.stroop-stim')) return false; if (document.querySelector('#jspsych-instructions-next')) return false; const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-content'); if (!el) return false; const t = el.innerText || ''; return t.includes('Press enter to begin') || t.includes('Press enter') || t.includes('take this time to read your feedback') || t.includes('test portion') || t.includes('Done with instructions'); })()

attention_check.response_js: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-attention-check-rdoc-stimulus'); if (!el) return null; const txt = el.innerText.toLowerCase(); const m = txt.match(/press the ([a-z]) key/); if (m) return m[1]; const ordinals = {'first':'a','second':'b','third':'c','third to last':'x','second to last':'y','last':'z'}; for (const k of Object.keys(ordinals)) { if (txt.includes(k + ' letter')) return ordinals[k]; } return 'a'; })()
